1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 082 — **two substantive prover lanes** building on the iter-081 in-flight unblocks; Lane 3 deferred pending Mathlib upstream).
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the path: Phase A (cohomology) + Phase B (differentials) + Phase C (Picard scheme, multi-step gap-fill — C0 substantially complete with `instIsMonoidal_W` deferred, C1 scheduled iter-083+, C2 iter-084+, C3 iter-086+) + Phase D (genus / Jacobian / instances, closed-by-reduction since iter-073) + Phase E (Abel–Jacobi, closed iter-073).
18	
19	Iter-081 ran two lanes; both made structural progress without regression but neither hit the closure target. Net 14 → 14. Highlights:
20	- **Lane 1 BasicOpenCech**: **S2+S3+S4 executed inline** atop iter-080's `letI` refactor. `have hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1 = n` plus 5-layer `dsimp` + full `simp [..., CochainComplex.of, ..., dif_pos hRel]` exposes the explicit alternating-sum form for both sides of the goal. Sorry shifted L1176 → L1196. **Key iter-081 correction**: the iter-080 recipe named `CochainComplex.of_d_eq_succ` for S3, but that lemma does NOT exist in Mathlib — actual bridge is `dif_pos hRel` inside a *full* `simp` invocation.
21	- **Lane 2 Differentials**: `_root_.PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_comp` landed fully closed at L454–465 (third permitted helper). `h_zero` of `cotangentExactSeq_structure` successfully closed in-flight via Route (c) — chain preserved as body comment block at L496–525. **New blocker for `h_epi`**: typeclass-coercion mismatch — cannot synthesize `Module ↑(X.presheaf.obj U) ↑((relativeDifferentials f).val.obj U)` because the natural instance is over the `forget₂`-image ring. Per the iter-081 conditional clause, body left as single absorbed `sorry` at L526 to preserve 5-sorry budget.
22	
23	Current project state (independently verified this plan pass via `sorry_analyzer.py` + `lean_diagnostic_messages`):
24	
25	| File | Sorries | Compiles? | Phase | Iter-082 plan |
26	|---|---|---|---|---|
27	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | 6 | yes | A | **Lane 1: complete Phase B (S5–S8 per-summand R-linearity) on top of iter-081's exposed alternating-sum form to close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1196)** |
28	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | 5 | yes | B | **Lane 2: reinstate verified Route (c) chain for `h_zero` + introduce `exact_iff_stalkwise` second gap-fill (sorry body, 1-for-1 shift) + close `h_epi` via Route 2 (identify descent with `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map`)** |
29	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` | 1 | yes | C0 | off-limits (deferred pending Mathlib upstream — `stalk_tensorObj` for varying-ring R₀) |
30	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | 1 | yes | D done | off-limits (`nonempty_jacobianWitness` deferred to iter-086+) |
31	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | yes | C-rep | off-limits |
32	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 0 | yes | E done | off-limits |
33	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` | 0 | yes | D done | off-limits |
34	| **Total syntactic** | **14** | — | | |
35	
36	## Current Objectives
37	
38	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.
39	
40	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — complete Phase B (S5–S8) to close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`
41	
42	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L502, L826, L854 (3 dead-end substeps — stay), **L1196 `h_diff_pi_smul_f`** (this lane's target), L1241 `g_R.map_smul'`, L1270 `h_loc_exact`.
43	
44	**Goal.** Close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1196) by completing Phase B (per-summand R-linearity) on top of iter-081's exposed alternating-sum form. After iter-081's S2+S3+S4 inline advance, BOTH sides of the goal at the sorry have the form:
45	```
46	(piIsoPi Z₂).hom (eqToHom _ ∘ₗ
47	    (∑ i : Fin (prev n + 2), (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (fun j' =>
48	       Pi.π Z₁ (j' ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom) ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j').op)))
49	  ((piIsoPi Z₁).symm <arg>) j
50	```
51	where `<arg>` is `r • y` on the LHS and `y` on the RHS (with an outer `r • ·` at the j-component level).
52	
53	**State to build on (DO NOT modify).** These iter-078/079/080/081 commits are essential prerequisites:
54	- `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L418 (iter-078).
55	- `intro r y; funext j` at L1102–1103 (iter-078).
56	- The named-per-i `letI` block at L920–949 (iter-080): `letI perI_n : ∀ i, Module R (Z_n i) := …; letI h_mod_pi_n : Module R (∀ i, Z_n i) := Pi.module _ _ _` for n = 1, 2, 3 — both `letI` (critical for typeclass visibility).
57	- **The iter-081 S2+S3+S4 chain at L1120–1153 (NEW iter-081)**: `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]`; then `have hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1 = n := by rcases n with _ | k · simp at hn · simp [ComplexShape.prev, ComplexShape.up_Rel]`; then `dsimp only [scK₀, K₀, cechCochain, cechComplexFunctor, toModuleKSheaf, toModuleKPresheaf_obj]`; then *full* `simp [FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor, .cosimplicialObjectFunctor, AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex, .obj, .objD, .map, CochainComplex.of, FormalCoproduct.evalOp, CosimplicialObject.δ, e₁, e₂, ModuleCat.piIsoPi, dif_pos hRel]`. This block is byte-for-byte equivalent to the iter-081 advance — **do NOT regress to an earlier shape**.
58	
59	**Mathematical content.** The statement `h_diff_pi_smul_f` packages: for `r : R` and `y : ∀ i, Z₁ i`, the f-side cochain differential `scK₀.f` applied to the smul `r • y` equals `r • (scK₀.f y)`, computed via the canonical `e_i : ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i) ≃ scK₀.X_i` linear equivalences. The R-action on `∀ i, Z_n i` is defined componentwise via `Pi.module`, where each `Z_n i = Γ(C.left, V_i)` is an R-algebra via the restriction map. After iter-081, the goal is in explicit alternating-sum form; the remaining Phase B work is per-summand R-linearity at fixed `i ∈ Fin (prev n + 2)`.
60	
61	**Recipe.**
62	
63	After the iter-081 S2+S3+S4 chain, the goal exposes the alternating sum on both sides. Continue with:
64	
65	- **S5 — Surface per-`(i, j')` summands at the j-component.** Chain:
66	  - `Pi.π Z₁ k (e₁.symm z) = z k` from `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι`. Reduces `(piIsoPi Z₁).symm z (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom)` to `z (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom)`.
67	  - `eqToHom_app`, `LinearMap.comp_apply`, `Finset.sum_apply` to commute the `eqToHom` cast through the sum and apply pointwise.
68	  - `Pi.lift_apply` to expose the per-`j'` form.
69	  Result: each summand at fixed `i` and output index `j` is `(-1)^i • ((toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op).hom (z (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom))` (LHS with `z = r • y`; RHS with `z = y` plus outer `r •`).
70	- **S6 — Distribute `r •` across RHS sum.** `Finset.smul_sum`; then `smul_comm` commutes the outer `r •` past `(-1)^i •` on each summand.
71	- **S7 — Per-summand R-linearity.** `apply Finset.sum_congr rfl; intro i _`.
72	  - LHS summand: `((toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op).hom ((r • y) (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom))`.
73	    - `(r • y) (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom) = (perI₁ (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom)).smul r (y (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom))` via `Pi.smul_apply` + the named-per-i `perI₁` from iter-080.
74	    - Unfold `perI₁`'s `RingHom.toModule.smul` to `(presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i} ≤ U).op).hom r * y (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom)`.
75	    - Apply `RingHom.map_mul` to split the `.hom` across the product: `((toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op).hom (a * b) = ((toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op).hom a * ((toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op).hom b`.
76	    - On `a = (presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i} ≤ U).op).hom r`: apply `← C.left.presheaf.map_comp` to collapse `presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_i}).op ∘ presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i} ≤ U).op = presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op`. Result: `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r`.
77	  - RHS summand: `r • ((toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op).hom (y (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom))` where the outer `r •` is `(perI₂ j).toSMul`, i.e., `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r * _`.
78	  - Both sides now: `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r * ((toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op).hom (y (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom))`.
79	- **S8** — close by `rfl` on each summand. The outer `Finset.sum_congr` then closes the whole goal.
80	
81	**Partial-credit fallback.** If S5 succeeds but S7 stalls on the `RingHom.map_mul` + `← presheaf.map_comp` step, leave the per-summand block as `?_` and document the per-summand goal in the body. This does NOT reduce the file sorry count but gives iter-083 a clean handoff. Do NOT stop early without documenting precisely what failed.
82	
83	**Approaches ruled out iter-076/078/079/080/081** (do NOT re-attempt):
84	- `rw [Pi.smul_apply]` against the anonymous-builder `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul` without the iter-080 refactor — "did not find the pattern".
85	- `letI` parallel instance to bypass `h_mod_pi₂` anonymity without making `h_mod_pi_n` itself a `letI`.
86	- `rfl` after S2's `dsimp` alone — sides are not defeq without the `(up ℕ).Rel` case-split + `dif_pos hRel` collapse.
87	- `exact map_smul (scK₀.f.hom) r _` — `scK₀.f` is k-linear, not R-linear (the R-linearity is the *construct*).
88	- `module` tactic — not a pure linear-combination shape; sides involve presheaf restriction maps.
89	- `simp only` (instead of full `simp`) for the S2+S3+S4 unfold — iter-081 confirmed fails.
90	- `CochainComplex.of_d_eq_succ` — does NOT exist in Mathlib (iter-081 verified). Use `dif_pos hRel` inside full `simp` instead.
91	- `rw [dif_pos hRel]` after a `simp only` chain — the conditional `dite` isn't yet in the goal.
92	
93	**Hard constraints**:
94	- **Preserve the iter-080 `letI` refactor at L920–949 byte-for-byte** (both `letI perI_n` AND `letI h_mod_pi_n := Pi.module _ _ _` for n = 1, 2, 3).
95	- **Preserve `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L418**.
96	- **Preserve iter-081's S2+S3+S4 chain at L1102–1153 byte-for-byte** (the `intro r y; funext j; simp only [Pi.smul_apply]` + `have hRel` + `dsimp only [...]` + full `simp [..., dif_pos hRel]` block). This is the iter-081 advance — regressing to an earlier shape forfeits the structural progress.
97	- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (user policy 2026-05-11). The S5–S8 chain must run inline inside `h_diff_pi_smul_f`'s body. No extraction into thin helpers across iterations.
98	- **No new axioms.**
99	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation** of candidate bodies. `lean_diagnostic_messages` after each save IS allowed (standard dev feedback loop). `lean_multi_attempt` for position-bound tactic-attempt previews is also allowed.
100	- Sorry-count budget: starts at 6; target **≤ 5** (close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`); hard cap **6** (no regression).
101	- The L502 / L826 / L854 sorries are dead-ends — out of scope.
102	- `g_R.map_smul'` (L1241) and `h_loc_exact` (L1270) are downstream — out of scope (gated on this lane).
103	
104	**Mathlib references confirmed iter-081** (verify via `lean_local_search`):
105	- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι` (S5 inverse projection).
106	- `Finset.sum_apply`, `Finset.smul_sum`, `Finset.sum_congr` (S5/S6/S7).

8	/-!
9	# Basic-open cover wrappers and the substantive Čech acyclicity theorem
10	
11	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 3) contains the `basicOpenCover` family
12	of definitions and identification lemmas (iter-054), the producer
13	`hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen` (iter-055), the
14	`cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt` lemma, and the
15	substantive theorem `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`
16	(iter-056 → iter-062). The latter currently carries two labelled substep
17	sorries plus the iter-062 `h_a_fun` scaffolding.
18	
19	This is cohort 3 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
20	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
21	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean`: 2-affine cover MV + cover-totality bridges +
22	  `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers + `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
23	* `BasicOpenCech.lean` (this file): basic-open cover wrappers + the

29	set_option autoImplicit false
30	
31	universe u v
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).
69	
70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80	
81	/-- Iter-054: each member of the basic-open cover of an affine open is itself
82	affine.
83	
84	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(i : s)`, conclude `IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover s i)`.
85	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
86	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L589).
87	
88	Used by iter-055+ when proving the Koszul acyclicity of the basic-open Čech
89	complex: each finite intersection of basic opens is itself basic (hence affine),
90	which is the inductive step for the Koszul-style alternating-sum argument. -/
91	theorem basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen
92	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
94	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i : s) :
95	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i) := by
96	  unfold basicOpenCover
97	  exact hU.basicOpen i.1
98	
99	/-- Iter-055: producer for `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` from existence of basic-open
100	Čech-acyclic cover.

400	
401	This is the structural skeleton common to *every* `IsCechAcyclicCover` instance
402	proof: the actual mathematical content lives in establishing
403	`(cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n` for each `n > 0`. -/
404	theorem cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt
405	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
406	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
407	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
408	    {n : ℕ} (h : (cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n) :
409	    Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n) :=
410	  ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero h.isZero_homology
411	
412	-- Iter-078: lifted to 800000 because the iter-078 prover work on
413	-- `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (per-summand R-linearity of the Čech differential)
414	-- requires further tactic steps inside the deeply-nested `have` block;
415	-- each additional rewrite touching the `(r • _)` smul triggers
416	-- `HSMul R (Z_i j) ?_` synthesis whose `whnf` cost exceeds the default
417	-- 200000 budget at the theorem head.
418	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
419	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
420	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
421	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
422	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
423	schemes in our setting.
424	
425	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
426	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
427	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
428	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
429	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
430	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
431	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
432	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
433	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
434	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
435	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
436	
437	The structural skeleton is:
438	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
439	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
440	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
441	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
442	
443	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
444	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
445	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
446	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
447	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
448	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
449	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
450	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
451	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
452	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
453	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
454	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
455	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
456	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
457	
458	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
459	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/
460	theorem basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf
461	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
462	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
463	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
464	    IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)
465	        (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) where
466	  subsingleton_cechCohomology n hn := by
467	    -- Step 0 (structural): reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to
468	    -- `ExactAt n` of the underlying Čech cochain complex via the iter-060
469	    -- helper `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`.
470	    refine cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt (n := n) ?_
471	    -- The goal is now
472	    --   `(cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)).ExactAt n`.
473	    -- Iter-061 decomposition: the Stacks 01ED extra-degeneracy + local-to-global
474	    -- argument has three substeps tracked by separate labelled `sorry`s.
475	    --
476	    -- ===========================================================================
477	    -- Substep (a) — Extra-degeneracy on the slice cover of `D(f)`.
478	    -- ===========================================================================
479	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover of `D(f)` by the family
480	    --   `{basicOpenCover s f' ⊓ D(f)}_{f' ∈ s}`
481	    -- contains `D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)` itself (at index `f' = f`), so the
482	    -- terminal vertex maps to `D(f)` via the identity section
483	    -- `D(f) → D(f)`. By `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`
484	    -- (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L92),
485	    -- the augmented Čech simplicial object on this slice has an
486	    -- `ExtraDegeneracy`. By `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
487	    -- (`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L328), the associated
488	    -- alternating-face-map chain complex is homotopy-equivalent to the
489	    -- constant complex on the augmentation. Op-passage through
490	    -- `CochainComplex.opEquivalence` then yields cochain-level exactness in
491	    -- positive degrees. We state substep (a) as a concrete `ExactAt n`
492	    -- claim on the slice cover Čech cochain complex:
493	    have h_a : ∀ (f : s),
494	        (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
495	            (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
496	              C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))).ExactAt n := by
497	      intro f
498	      -- The terminal vertex of the slice cover is at `f' = f`:
499	      --   `basicOpenCover s f ⊓ C.left.basicOpen f.1 = D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)`.
500	      -- Plug into `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` with `i₀ := f`,
501	      -- extract `homotopyEquiv`, op-pass, read off `ExactAt n` for `0 < n`.
502	      sorry  -- substep (a) infrastructure: augmented Čech simplicial object
503	             -- + extraDegeneracyCech + homotopyEquiv + CochainComplex.opEquivalence
504	    -- ===========================================================================
505	    -- Substep (b) — Localised-Čech identification.
506	    -- ===========================================================================
507	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover Čech cochain complex above is
508	    -- isomorphic (in `ModuleCat k`) to the localisation at `f` of the
509	    -- original Čech cochain complex `K`. Cochain-factor-wise:
510	    --   `(slice cover).X^n = ∏_x F(⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k) ⊓ D(f))`
511	    --                      = `∏_x F(D(f · ∏ k, (x k).1))`              (Stacks identity)
512	    --                      = `∏_x Localization.Away (f · ∏ k, (x k).1) Γ(C.left, U)`
513	    --                      = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k))[1/f]`
514	    --                      = `K.X^n [1/f]`.
515	    -- The first equality is iter-057 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod`)
516	    -- applied to the augmented index `{f} ∪ image(x)`. The second is
517	    -- iter-059 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization`). The third is
518	    -- the universal property of localisation (transitivity of localisation:
519	    -- `R[1/(f·g)] = R[1/g][1/f]` when `f` is also among the generators).
520	    -- The differential identification uses `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`
521	    -- (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Exact.lean` L56) to transport
522	    -- the cochain-level identification to the slice differential.
523	    --
524	    -- ===========================================================================
525	    -- Substep (c) — Local-to-global via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`.
526	    -- ===========================================================================
527	    -- The combined data from substeps (a) and (b) — per-`f` exactness at
528	    -- degree `n` of the slice cover Čech complex, which by substep (b) is
529	    -- the localisation of `K.sc n` — fits the hypothesis of
530	    -- `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ...`
531	    -- (`Mathlib/RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean` L173) viewing
532	    -- the cochain factors as `Γ(C.left, U)`-modules through their natural
533	    -- module structure. The conclusion is `Function.Exact ⇑(K.sc n).f ⇑(K.sc n).g`,
534	    -- which converts back to `K.ExactAt n` via
535	    -- `ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact` and
536	    -- `HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff`.
537	    --
538	    -- This combined assembly remains as a single transient sorry: it
539	    -- requires constructing the cochain-level localisation iso (substep (b)
540	    -- ≈ a `noncomputable def` for the localised Čech short complex plus
541	    -- its identification iso) and routing the ring-base mismatch
542	    -- (`(K.sc n).f.hom` is `k`-linear; `exact_of_isLocalized_span` consumes
543	    -- `Γ(C.left, U)`-linear maps — they coincide as set-functions, but the
544	    -- type-level reconciliation is non-trivial).
545	    --
546	    -- For iter-061, substep (a)'s claim is concretely stated above and
547	    -- substep (b)+(c)'s assembly remains as the second labelled sorry below.
548	    -- We perform the mechanical conversion `K.ExactAt n ↔ Function.Exact ⇑f ⇑g`
549	    -- before the sorry, so the remaining content is precisely the
550	    -- "transport per-f exactness through the localisation iso then apply
551	    -- the local-to-global principle" assembly step.
552	    rw [HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff,
553	        ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact]
554	    -- Goal:
555	    --   `Function.Exact ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((K).sc n).f)
556	    --                   ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((K).sc n).g)`
557	    -- where `K := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)`.
558	    -- ===========================================================================
559	    -- Iter-062 substantive scaffolding (a): convert `h_a` into `Function.Exact` form.
560	    -- ===========================================================================
561	    -- The same `HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff` +
562	    -- `ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact` chain that
563	    -- converts our goal also converts each per-`f` slice-cover `ExactAt n`
564	    -- hypothesis into a per-`f` `Function.Exact` statement at the
565	    -- function-level. This is closed under `← rw` (no new sorry).
566	    have h_a_fun : ∀ (f : s),
567	        Function.Exact
568	          ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc
569	              (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
570	                (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
571	                  C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))) n).f)
572	          ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc
573	              (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
574	                (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
575	                  C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))) n).g) := by
576	      intro f
577	      rw [← ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact,
578	          ← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff]
579	      exact h_a f
580	    -- ===========================================================================
581	    -- Iter-062 substantive scaffolding (b): outline the local-to-global route.
582	    -- ===========================================================================
583	    -- The closure of the remaining goal proceeds via Mathlib's local-to-global
584	    -- principle for exactness `exact_of_isLocalized_span` applied to the
585	    -- ring `R := Γ(C.left, U)`, the spanning subset `s ⊆ R`, and the
586	    -- `R`-linear maps `(K.sc n).f.hom` and `(K.sc n).g.hom` (treated as
587	    -- `R`-linear via the `Module R ((K.sc n).X i)`-structure obtained from
588	    -- the cochain factor's natural `Γ(C.left, ⋂_x …)`-module structure
589	    -- precomposed with the algebra map `R → Γ(C.left, ⋂_x …)`).
590	    -- The required input is, for each `f ∈ s`, a `Function.Exact`
591	    -- statement on the localised cochain factors at `f`. By the
592	    -- cochain-factor identification chain (iter-057 + iter-058 + iter-059
593	    -- + Mathlib `Localization.localizationLocalization` + product-localisation
594	    -- commutation), the localised `(K.sc n).X i [1/f]` identifies with the
595	    -- slice-cover Čech `.X i` at the same degree, and consequently the
596	    -- localised differential identifies with the slice-cover differential.
597	    -- This identification + `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` transports
598	    -- `h_a_fun f` (which is the slice-cover Čech `Function.Exact` at
599	    -- degree `n`) to `Function.Exact` of the localised differential.
600	    -- The infinite-set subtlety (`s` is `Set`, not `Finset`) is sidestepped
601	    -- because `Ideal.span s = ⊤` already implies a *finite* subset spans
602	    -- the unit ideal — `exact_of_isLocalized_span` only consumes the per-`r`
603	    -- localised exactness data and the spanning hypothesis, not finiteness
604	    -- of `s` directly.
605	    -- ===========================================================================
606	    -- Substep (b1) — install the `Module Γ(C.left, U)` instances on each
607	    -- cochain factor `(K.sc n).X i`.
608	    -- ===========================================================================
609	    -- The cochain factor `K.X i = ∏_{x : Fin (i+1) → s} Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k))`
610	    -- carries a `Γ(C.left, U)`-module structure pointwise, since each
611	    -- `Γ(C.left, V)` for `V ≤ U` is a `Γ(C.left, U)`-algebra via the
612	    -- restriction map (iter-058's `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le` supplies
613	    -- the inclusion `V ≤ U`, iter-006's `algebraSection` supplies the
614	    -- algebra structure on each factor).
615	    --
616	    -- ===========================================================================
617	    -- Substep (b2) — exhibit `slice_K.X i` as `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1`
618	    -- of `(K.sc n).X i` for each `f ∈ s`.
619	    -- ===========================================================================
620	    -- The cochain-factor identification chain (iter-057 + iter-058 + iter-059
621	    -- + Mathlib `Localization.localizationLocalization` + product-localisation
622	    -- commutation) gives
623	    --   `slice_K.X i = ∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰_slice(x k))`
624	    --                = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k) ⊓ D(f))`
625	    --                = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k))[1/f]`        (iter-058 + iter-059)
626	    --                = `(∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k)))[1/f]`     (product-loc commutation)
627	    --                = `(K.X i)[1/f]`.
628	    -- The pointwise localisation map (component-wise restriction map
629	    -- `Γ(C.left, V) → Γ(C.left, V')` for `V' = V ⊓ D(f) ≤ V`) is the canonical
630	    -- localisation map and an `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` instance.
631	    --
632	    -- ===========================================================================
633	    -- Substep (c) — apply `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ... h_a_fun`.
634	    -- ===========================================================================
635	    -- With the data from (b1) and (b2), `exact_of_isLocalized_span` packages
636	    -- the per-`f` exactness `h_a_fun f` into the global `Function.Exact`
637	    -- conclusion. The remaining work is purely the unification of the per-`f`
638	    -- input shape with the conclusion of `IsLocalizedModule.map ... F`
639	    -- via the iso from (b2) and `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`.
640	    -- ===========================================================================
641	    -- Iter-063 progress: explicit finite-subspanning extraction + obstruction
642	    -- diagnosis.
643	    -- ===========================================================================
644	    -- The localisation-commutes-with-products subtlety identified by the
645	    -- iter-061 prover (Attempt 3) is genuine and severe: for the cochain
646	    -- factor `K.X i = ∏ᶜ_{x : Fin (i+1) → s} P.obj (op (∏ᶜ_k 𝒰(x k)))` in
647	    -- `ModuleCat k` (where `P` is the underlying presheaf of `toModuleKSheaf C`),
648	    -- the index set `Fin (i+1) → s` is *infinite* when `s` is infinite, and
649	    -- `LocalizedModule (powers f) (∏ᶜ_x M_x) ≄ ∏ᶜ_x LocalizedModule (powers f) M_x`
650	    -- in general for infinite products. So `slice_K.X i = ∏_x Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f))`
651	    -- is NOT literally `(K.X i)[1/f]` — the natural map between them is
652	    -- *not* an `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1`. Iter-062's substep (b2) plan
653	    -- ("install `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` instances on the product")
654	    -- requires reducing to a finite index-set first.
655	    --
656	    -- Iter-063 lands the finite-subspanning extraction as a structural fact
657	    -- inside the proof: `Ideal.span s = ⊤` implies the existence of a
658	    -- *finite* subset `s₀ ⊆ s` with `Ideal.span ↑s₀ = ⊤` via Mathlib's
659	    -- `Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite`. The remaining substep work uses `s₀`
660	    -- (a `Finset`, hence `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀` is finite) to install the
661	    -- product-localisation commutation. The transport step from `s₀`-cover
662	    -- exactness to `s`-cover exactness is itself non-trivial: it requires
663	    -- a Čech-complex refinement comparison (`s₀ ⊆ s` ⇒ Čech-cohomology

63:theorem piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype (i : ι) :

40	  lift s := ofHom (LinearMap.pi fun j => (s.π.app ⟨j⟩).hom : s.pt →ₗ[R] ∀ i : ι, Z i)

46	-- While we could use this to construct a `HasProducts (ModuleCat R)` instance,
47	-- we already have `HasLimits (ModuleCat R)` in `Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Limits`.
48	variable [HasProduct Z]
49	
50	/-- The categorical product of a family of objects in `ModuleCat`
51	agrees with the usual module-theoretical product.
52	-/
53	noncomputable def piIsoPi : ∏ᶜ Z ≅ ModuleCat.of R (∀ i, Z i) :=
54	  limit.isoLimitCone ⟨_, productConeIsLimit Z⟩
55	
56	-- We now show this isomorphism commutes with the inclusion of the kernel into the source.
57	@[simp, elementwise]
58	theorem piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι (i : ι) :
59	    (piIsoPi Z).inv ≫ Pi.π Z i = ofHom (LinearMap.proj i : (∀ i : ι, Z i) →ₗ[R] Z i) :=
60	  limit.isoLimitCone_inv_π _ _
61	
62	@[simp, elementwise]
63	theorem piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype (i : ι) :
64	    (piIsoPi Z).hom ≫ ofHom (LinearMap.proj i : (∀ i : ι, Z i) →ₗ[R] Z i) = Pi.π Z i :=
65	  IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_inv_comp _ (limit.isLimit _) (Discrete.mk i)
66	
67	end product

75	/-- The coproduct cone induced by the concrete coproduct. -/
76	def coproductCocone : Cofan Z :=
77	  Cofan.mk (ModuleCat.of R (⨁ i : ι, Z i)) fun i => ofHom (DirectSum.lof R ι (fun i ↦ Z i) i)

248	membership) to arbitrary non-empty Finset indexing. Used by iter-058+ Čech
249	evaluation: each finite intersection of basic opens of an affine is itself
250	affine, which is the inductive step for the Koszul alternating-sum argument
251	(Stacks 03F7 / Hartshorne III.4.5 / Eisenbud Commutative Algebra §17 Koszul
252	resolution).
253	
254	**Body uses `▸` (Eq.mpr), NOT `rw`**: `rw` on the equation produced by Theorem
255	(1) fails because of motive-occurrence issues with the implicit `Subtype.val`
256	coercions on elements of `Finset s`; term-mode `▸` rewrite resolves these
257	directly without invoking the rewriter's pattern-matching. Plan-agent
258	`lean_run_code` probe (this pass) confirmed the term-mode form compiles
259	kernel-only while the tactic-mode `rw` form fails with "Did not find an
260	occurrence of the pattern" error. -/
261	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen
262	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
263	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
264	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
265	    IsAffineOpen (t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)) :=
266	  basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod s t h ▸ hU.basicOpen _
267	
268	/-- Iter-058: n-ary basic-open intersection is contained in `U`.
269	
270	Given a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`, the infimum (over `t`) of the basic-open
271	cover members `basicOpenCover s i` for `i ∈ t` is contained in `U`. Term-mode
272	body chains iter-057's `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod` (rewriting
273	the n-ary intersection as a single basic open of the finite product) with
274	Mathlib's `Scheme.basicOpen_le` (`X.basicOpen f ≤ U` for any `f ∈ Γ(X, U)`)
275	via Lean's `▸` (Eq.mpr) rewrite.
276	
277	**No `IsAffineOpen` hypothesis required**: `basicOpen_le` holds for any open `U`
278	(the basic open of `f ∈ Γ(X, U)` is always a sub-open of `U`); compare iter-057's
279	`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen` which does need `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)`.
280	
281	This is the inclusion morphism iter-059+ Čech-evaluation arguments need when
282	applying Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`:
283	```
284	(hU : IsAffineOpen U) (f : Γ(X, U)) {V : X.Opens} (i : V ⟶ U) :
285	  V = X.basicOpen f → IsLocalization.Away f Γ(X, V)
286	```
287	The morphism `i : V ⟶ U` argument (which is `homOfLE` of `V ≤ U`) is needed to
288	install the `Algebra Γ(X, U) Γ(X, V)` instance for the conclusion type, since
289	Mathlib's `algebra_section_section_basicOpen` instance only fires for the
290	syntactic form `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)`, not for `V = t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)`.
291	Iter-058's inclusion lemma supplies this `V ≤ U` proof so iter-059+ can call
292	`hU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen f (homOfLE (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le ...))
293	(basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod ...)` to land
294	`IsLocalization.Away (∏ i ∈ t, i.1) Γ(C.left, t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s))`
295	directly. -/
296	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le
297	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
298	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
299	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
300	    t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) ≤ U :=
301	  basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod s t h ▸
302	    C.left.basicOpen_le _
303	
304	/-- Iter-059: n-ary basic-open intersection is a localization.
305	
306	Given an affine open `U`, a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, and a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`,
307	the structure sheaf evaluated on the n-ary intersection of basic-open cover members
308	`t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)` is identified — as a localization away from the product
309	`∏ i ∈ t, i.1` of generators — with `Γ(C.left, U)`. The algebra structure on the conclusion
310	type is given explicitly by `(C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE _).op).hom.toAlgebra`, threaded
311	through the conclusion `@IsLocalization.Away _ _ f Γ(X, V) _ algebra` form (matching
312	Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` signature exactly).
313	
314	**Explicit `@`-algebra binding is essential.** Mathlib's default
315	`algebra_section_section_basicOpen` instance (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Scheme.lean` L724)
316	fires only for the syntactic form `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)`, *not* for arbitrary `V`
317	definitionally equal to `X.basicOpen f`. The iter-058 plan-agent probe-confirmed that
318	stating this theorem with *inferred* algebra fails with
319	`failed to synthesize instance of type class Algebra Γ(C.left, U) Γ(C.left, t.inf' h …)`.
320	Threading the algebra structure as part of the conclusion type — matching Mathlib's
321	signature exactly — sidesteps the synthesis attempt.
322	
323	This is the **fourth and final** thin foundational scaffolding helper on the affine
324	basic-open identification side. The iter-060+ basic-open Koszul acyclicity argument
325	(per the iter-059 analogy report at `analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`, Branch A
326	substep 2) chains iter-057's equality + iter-058's inclusion + iter-059's localization
327	+ `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Exact.lean`)
328	to identify each Čech-cochain factor `F(⋂_k 𝒰(x k))` with the cover-of-`D(f)` Čech complex
329	on localised modules. -/
330	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization
331	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
332	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
333	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
334	    @IsLocalization.Away _ _ (∏ i ∈ t, (i.1 : Γ(C.left, U)))
335	      Γ(C.left, t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)) _
336	      ((C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE
337	        (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le (C := C) (U := U) s t h)).op).hom.toAlgebra) :=
338	  hU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen _
339	    (homOfLE (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le (C := C) (U := U) s t h))
340	    (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod (C := C) (U := U) s t h)
341	
342	/-- Iter-066 helper: in `ModuleCat k`, a product projection induced by an
343	injection of index sets is a split epi.
344	
345	Given an injection `f : β → α` and a family `M : α → ModuleCat k`, the natural
346	projection `∏ᶜ_a M a ⟶ ∏ᶜ_b M (f b)` defined as `Pi.lift (fun b => Pi.π M (f b))`
347	(which sends a tuple indexed by `α` to its `image f` components, reindexed by `β`)
348	admits a section in `ModuleCat k`. The section is the "extend by zero" map:
349	given a tuple indexed by `β`, fill in the components in `image f` (using the
350	unique preimage given by injectivity of `f`) and set the remaining components
351	to `0`.
352	
353	This is the categorical fact underlying `h_π_split` in
354	`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`: the cochain-complex map
355	`π : K ⟶ K₀` is at each degree of this form (the `↑s₀ ↪ s` inclusion induces a
356	postcomposition map `(Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀) → (Fin (i+1) → s)` which is injective by
357	injectivity of the inclusion, hence the induced product projection on cochain
358	factors is split).
359	
360	The helper is stated for a generic injection so it can be reused for both the
361	`s` substep (`h_π_split` proper) and the slice-cover variants. -/
362	noncomputable def splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective {k : Type u} [Field k]
363	    {α β : Type u} (M : α → ModuleCat.{u} k)
364	    (f : β → α) (hf : Function.Injective f) :
365	    SplitEpi (Pi.lift (fun b : β => Pi.π M (f b))) where
366	  section_ := by
367	    classical
368	    refine Pi.lift (fun a => ?_)
369	    by_cases h : ∃ b, f b = a
370	    · exact h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π (fun b => M (f b)) h.choose
371	    · exact 0
372	  id := by
373	    classical
374	    apply Pi.hom_ext
375	    intro b
376	    rw [Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, Pi.lift_π]
377	    have h : ∃ b', f b' = f b := ⟨b, rfl⟩
378	    rw [dif_pos h]
379	    -- The transport `h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π _ h.choose = Pi.π _ b`.
380	    -- By injectivity of `f`, `h.choose = b`, so the transport collapses.
381	    -- We use the helper lemma below.
382	    have key : ∀ {b b' : β} (heq : f b' = f b),
383	        heq ▸ (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b' :
384	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b')) =
385	          (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b :
386	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b)) := by
387	      intro b b' heq
388	      have hbb : b' = b := hf heq
389	      subst hbb
390	      rfl
391	    exact key h.choose_spec
392	
393	/-- Iter-060 helper: bridge from `ExactAt n` on the Čech cochain complex to
394	`Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)`.
395	
396	The chain is two steps: `HomologicalComplex.ExactAt.isZero_homology` packages an
397	`ExactAt n` hypothesis into `IsZero ((cechCochain ...).homology n)`, and
398	`ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero` then upgrades `IsZero` on a `ModuleCat`
399	object to `Subsingleton` on its underlying carrier type.
400	
401	This is the structural skeleton common to *every* `IsCechAcyclicCover` instance
402	proof: the actual mathematical content lives in establishing
403	`(cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n` for each `n > 0`. -/
404	theorem cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt
405	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
406	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
407	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
408	    {n : ℕ} (h : (cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n) :
409	    Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n) :=
410	  ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero h.isZero_homology
411	
412	-- Iter-078: lifted to 800000 because the iter-078 prover work on
413	-- `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (per-summand R-linearity of the Čech differential)
414	-- requires further tactic steps inside the deeply-nested `have` block;
415	-- each additional rewrite touching the `(r • _)` smul triggers
416	-- `HSMul R (Z_i j) ?_` synthesis whose `whnf` cost exceeds the default
417	-- 200000 budget at the theorem head.
418	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
419	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
420	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
421	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
422	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
423	schemes in our setting.
424	
425	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
426	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
427	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
428	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
429	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
430	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
431	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
432	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
433	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
434	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
435	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
436	
437	The structural skeleton is:
438	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
439	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
440	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
441	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
442	
443	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
444	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
445	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
446	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
447	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
448	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
449	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
450	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
451	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
452	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
453	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
454	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
455	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
456	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
457	
458	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
459	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/
460	theorem basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf
461	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
462	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
463	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
464	    IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)
465	        (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) where
466	  subsingleton_cechCohomology n hn := by
467	    -- Step 0 (structural): reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to
468	    -- `ExactAt n` of the underlying Čech cochain complex via the iter-060
469	    -- helper `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`.
470	    refine cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt (n := n) ?_
471	    -- The goal is now
472	    --   `(cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)).ExactAt n`.
473	    -- Iter-061 decomposition: the Stacks 01ED extra-degeneracy + local-to-global
474	    -- argument has three substeps tracked by separate labelled `sorry`s.
475	    --
476	    -- ===========================================================================
477	    -- Substep (a) — Extra-degeneracy on the slice cover of `D(f)`.
478	    -- ===========================================================================
479	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover of `D(f)` by the family
480	    --   `{basicOpenCover s f' ⊓ D(f)}_{f' ∈ s}`
481	    -- contains `D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)` itself (at index `f' = f`), so the
482	    -- terminal vertex maps to `D(f)` via the identity section
483	    -- `D(f) → D(f)`. By `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`
484	    -- (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L92),
485	    -- the augmented Čech simplicial object on this slice has an
486	    -- `ExtraDegeneracy`. By `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
487	    -- (`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L328), the associated
488	    -- alternating-face-map chain complex is homotopy-equivalent to the
489	    -- constant complex on the augmentation. Op-passage through
490	    -- `CochainComplex.opEquivalence` then yields cochain-level exactness in
491	    -- positive degrees. We state substep (a) as a concrete `ExactAt n`
492	    -- claim on the slice cover Čech cochain complex:
493	    have h_a : ∀ (f : s),
494	        (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
495	            (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
496	              C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))).ExactAt n := by
497	      intro f
498	      -- The terminal vertex of the slice cover is at `f' = f`:
499	      --   `basicOpenCover s f ⊓ C.left.basicOpen f.1 = D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)`.

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean — iter-082

## Status

- File compiles cleanly (no errors).
- Sorry count: **6** (unchanged from iter-081 entry; budget hard cap 6).
- Sorry locations (post-edit):
  - L502 — substep (a) extra-degeneracy on `s`-indexed slice cover (dead-end, out of scope).
  - L826 — slice/refinement transport (dead-end, out of scope).
  - L854 — substep (a) for `s₀` (dead-end, out of scope).
  - **L1240** — `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (this lane's target; advanced from L1196 with S5 prelude inline).
  - L1285 — `g_R.map_smul'` (gated downstream, out of scope).
  - L1314 — `h_loc_exact` (gated downstream, out of scope).

## h_diff_pi_smul_f (L1240) — Lane 1 work

### Attempt 1 (this iter): S5 prelude — push `j`-projection past `piIsoPi Z₂`

- **Approach:**
  1. Re-fold `limit.isoLimitCone ⟨productCone, productConeIsLimit⟩` (which the iter-081 `simp` exposed) into the `ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z_i` API form via two `rw [show ... = ... from rfl]` steps. This is a syntactic re-association — no semantic change.
  2. Bridge the `(piIsoPi Z₂).toLinearEquiv x` form (which doesn't match piIsoPi simp lemmas) to `(ConcreteCategory.hom (piIsoPi Z₂).hom) x` via a `show` step.
  3. Apply `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply` twice (once per side) to push the `j`-projection past the iso, replacing `(piIsoPi Z₂).hom z j` with `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom z`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED for S5 prelude. Goal at L1240 is now in shape:
  ```
  (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ((eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ Σ_hom) ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv (r • y)))
    = r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ((eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ Σ_hom) ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv y))
  ```
  with the `j`-projection now adjacent to the differential, ready for per-summand R-linearity work S6–S8.
- **Sorry count delta:** unchanged (6 → 6); structural advance documented in body comments at L1167–1196.

### Attempt 2 (this iter): split `(eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ Σ_hom) z` into `eqToHom_hom (Σ_hom z)`

- **Approach:** Use `LinearMap.comp_apply` (Mathlib `Algebra/Module/LinearMap/Defs` L501) to split the LinearMap composition.
- **Result:** FAILED. The Mathlib `LinearMap.comp_apply` is stated for `(f ∘ₛₗ g) x` (semilinear notation), while our goal has the homogeneous `∘ₗ` notation. Higher-order unification doesn't bridge them despite definitional equality.
- **Workarounds tried (all FAILED):**
  - `simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]` — no progress; the `(... ∘ₗ ...) z` form has no explicit `⇑(... ∘ₗ ...)` coercion to rewrite.
  - `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]` — same `∘ₛₗ` vs `∘ₗ` pattern issue.
  - `rfl` — sides not defeq (the smul on Z₂ at j is via `perI₂ j` while the LHS goes through the differential, so the equality is non-trivial).
  - `dsimp only []` — no progress.
  - `change` to explicitly unfolded form — fails because `eqToHom`'s implicit type proof (`eqToHom ⋯` in the goal) is not directly displayable in surface syntax.
- **Dead-end warning for next iteration:** Do NOT spend cycles on `LinearMap.comp_apply` rewrites against the homogeneous `∘ₗ` notation — the unification fails. The path forward must either (a) introduce a named `let M : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) →ₗ[k] ↑(∏ᶜ Z₂) := eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ Σ_hom` abbreviation BEFORE the iter-081 simp so the comp lives behind a definition (then `M` itself can be promoted to R-linear via per-summand reasoning); or (b) take the per-summand approach DIRECTLY without splitting the comp first (use `Pi.lift_apply` and `Finset.sum_apply` / `Finset.smul_sum` from a different angle).

### Mathlib lemmas verified relevant (via `lean_loogle`)

- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply`: `(piIsoPi Z).hom x i = (Pi.π Z i) x` (key lemma for S5).
- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι_apply`: `(Pi.π Z i) ((piIsoPi Z).inv x) = x i` (needed for S7 inner projection).
- `ModuleCat.Iso.toLinearEquiv_apply`: `i.toLinearEquiv x = i.hom x` (rfl bridge between LinearEquiv and Iso application).
- `LinearMap.comp_apply`: `f.comp g x = f (g x)` — verified to NOT fire as `rw` due to `∘ₛₗ` pattern (despite the goal having `∘ₗ`, which is `LinearMap.comp` with `RingHom.id`).

### Next-iteration recipe (iter-083+)

After S5 prelude (already executed), the goal at the L1240 sorry is:
```
(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ((eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ Σ_hom) ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv (r • y)))
  = r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ((eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ Σ_hom) ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv y))
```
where the inner sum is `∑ i : Fin (prev n + 2), (-1)^i • Pi.lift (fun j' => Pi.π Z₁ (j'∘δ_i.toOrderHom) ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map φ.op)`.

**Recommended next-iteration strategy (path (a) above):** Refactor the iter-081 `simp [..., dif_pos hRel]` chain to NOT collapse the comp into a syntactic `∘ₗ`. Instead, use `dsimp only` + targeted rewrites that expose the alternating sum WITHOUT the `eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ` outer wrapper. Alternative: introduce `let M : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) ⟶ ↑(∏ᶜ Z₂) := eqToHom _ ≫ Σ` (categorical comp inside ModuleCat, not LinearMap.comp), then evaluate at the j-component via `Pi.π_eq_lift`-style unfolds.

**Alternative path (b):** Per-summand R-linearity. After the S5 prelude:
1. `LinearMap.coe_sum`, `Finset.sum_apply` to commute `Pi.π Z₂ j` past the sum (HARD because the `eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ` wrapping prevents direct sum-distribution).
2. `LinearMap.coe_smul`, `Finset.smul_sum` for the `r • Σ` distribution.
3. `apply Finset.sum_congr rfl; intro i _` for per-summand reduction.
4. Each summand: `Pi.lift_apply` + `Pi.π Z₁ k ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv z) = z k` (via `piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι_apply`) to extract the projection. Then RingHom.map_mul + ← presheaf.map_comp to identify both sides per-summand.

The per-summand argument is ~30 LOC but each rewrite shifts the deeply-nested type structure; expect 3–5 iterations of focused work to land it.
